reconstructibility_reasoning: From the chapter context alone (without 2.36), an engineering analog focused on 'enemies who already wished failure rewriting earned prowess in the architecture-review channel' is not trivially reconstructible. The chapter context up to 2.35 establishes svadharma, the closing of escape routes, the formerly-esteemed retraction of esteem. The 2.36 step — specifically that ENEMIES (not formerly-esteemed) will speak indecent words about EARNED prowess (not just the action) — is a distinct intensification. The named-narrator analysis as the operational tool, the Postgres-to-Aurora migration as the concrete scenario, and the architecture-review channel as the load-bearing forum are 2.36-specific anchors that do not generalize. PASS.
